name: Gold
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **GOLD $4,711.30/oz MAY 12 (-0.2% 1D, -0.7% 1M, -4.3% 3M) — STILL UNDERPERFORMING ON CRISIS DAY.** Expected on a day with VIX +6.9%, INR record low, Sensex -1.7%, Brent +1.3% on crisis news: gold should print +1-2%. Got: -0.2%. **WHY UNDERPERFORMING (UNCHANGED REGIME READ):** US 10Y yield 4.41% (+1.1% 1D, +2.2% 1M) — oil-driven inflation expectations keep real yields competitive vs zero-coupon gold; DXY +0.2% (modest USD strength on safe-haven flow INTO USD vs gold); cycle gold rally Mar-Apr to ~$5,050 → -6.7% off peak; COMEX positioning crowded. **ASSESSMENT: NO REGIME CHANGE FROM YESTERDAY'S READ. Gold is signaling this is an INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel), NOT a financial-system-stress event (which would force gold above $5,000).** Cleanest reversal signal would be: Gulf-exposed bank reports unexpected Q1 loss, or European-bank with ME exposure shows CDS stress. Neither visible. **Gold remains a tired long.** **B8 PM MODI MAY 10-11 HYDERABAD ADDRESS asked Indians to DEFER GOLD PURCHASES (incl. weddings) FOR ONE YEAR** — targets India's ~$50bn/yr gold import drain (FX-pressure relief), but the demand-shock implication for gold itself is muted: India's wedding-season gold buying is 600-700t per year; even a 30-50% deferral would shave 200-300t = ~3-5% of global ~6,000t mine output. Modest bearish overhang. **TRIGGER WATCH: If Brent breaks $115 AND VIX >22, gold should make fresh run at $4,850. Until then, real yields dominate.** Silver $85.42 (-0.1% 1D, +13.1% 1M) — industrial-demand component (solar/EV) holding; gold/silver bifurcation continues. Copper $6.46/lb (+0.7% 1D, +8.1% 1M, +11.9% 3M) — AI infra + supply-side tightness; bid intact. Platinum -0.9% 1D; Palladium -0.9% 1D / -4.4% 1M (auto-demand weakness).

current.regime: TIRED LONG — gold signaling INFLATION + FLOW event (negative via real-yield channel), NOT financial-system stress event. UNDERPERFORMING ON CRISIS DAY MAY 12: with VIX +6.9%, INR record low, Sensex -1.7%, Brent +1.3%, gold should print +1-2% — got -0.2%. Real yields at 4.41% (10Y +1.1% 1D) competitive vs gold; DXY +0.2% adds modest pressure. COMEX positioning crowded after earlier rally to $5,050 = -6.7% off peak. Modi May 10-11 'defer gold purchases for 1 year' appeal adds modest bearish demand-overhang. Cleanest reversal trigger: Gulf-bank or European-bank with ME exposure shows stress = gold breaks $5,000+ within days. Until then, TIRED.
current.watching_threshold: Gulf-bank or European-bank with ME exposure shows stress = gold breaks $5,000+ within days. Until then, TIRED. Pre-crisis Feb baseline ~$2,920; March-Apr highs ~$5,050; current $4,720 = -6.5% from peak. Bifurcation in metals complex: copper +2.0% / +8.7% MoM bidding on AI infra; gold/silver/platinum stalling — confirms inflation/flow regime over financial-stress regime.

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — GOLD $4,730.70 MAY 8 CLOSE per markets dossier (+0.7% 1D, -1.3% 1M, -5.5% 3M); STILL UNDERPERFORMING DESPITE ACCUMULATING UNPRICED CRISIS NEWS (Sunday bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike, Kharg satellite slick, Bahrain 41 IRGC detentions, UAE engagement scale refined, Hezbollah-IDF Day 14). REGIME ASSESSMENT: gold continues to signal INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel) and NOT financial-system stress event. VIX 17.19 cycle-low + S&P record + US 10Y at 4.36% + crisis events fail to bid gold = inflation/flow regime over financial-stress regime confirmed. **TIRED LONG until a Gulf-bank or European-bank with ME exposure shows stress = gold breaks $5,000+ within days.** Metals complex bifurcating: copper $6.30/lb (+2.8% 1D, +9.5% 1M) bidding on AI infra demand; gold/silver/platinum stalling. Silver $80.86 (+1.5% 1D, +6.0% 1M); copper +2.8% 1D / +9.5% 1M (China stimulus + AI infra); palladium -2.0% 1D (auto-demand weakness). **The non-mover IS the signal — if markets actually feared kinetic restart, gold would be at $5,000+. Instead, gold flat-to-down while AI semis +30% MoM tells you the marginal $1 of geopolitical-hedge capital is going to Korean memory, not bullion.** Decision-relevant: if MOU signs, gold continues flat-to-down; if Iran rejects past May 21, gold gets war-risk re-bid back toward $4,850-5,000.

recent_signals.headline: GOLD $4,720.40 MAY 8 CLOSE (+0.4% 1D, -1.5% 1M, -6.5% 3M) — STILL UNDERPERFORMING ON CRISIS DAY (HORMUZ DAY 71, MAY 7 DESTROYER ATTACK, MAY 8 SECOND TANKER DISABLING + CENTCOM 70-TANKER BLOCKADE SCALE + MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' SPEECH); EXPECTED HARD BID, ACTUAL +0.4%. **REGIME ASSESSMENT: gold signaling INFLATION + FLOW event (negative for gold via real-yield channel), NOT financial-system stress event.** US 10Y at 4.36% kept real yields competitive vs gold; gold rallied to ~$5,050 March-April peak then -6.5% 3M = exhaustion at top. VIX cycle-low + US 10Y not making new highs + S&P at record reinforces this read. **TIRED LONG until a Gulf-bank or European-bank with ME exposure shows stress = gold breaks $5,000+ within days.** Metals complex bifurcating: copper +2.0% 1D / +8.7% 1M (AI infra demand); gold/silver/platinum stalling. The '$4,600 sustained 3+ sessions' trigger long resolved — refocusing on Gulf-bank-stress trigger.

recent_signals.headline: GOLD $4,732.40 (+1.1%) — DAY 2 OF '$4,600 SUSTAINED 3+ SESSIONS' TRIGGER ON TRACK. DOLLAR-WEAKNESS REGIME INTACT (DXY 98.03, Rieder Fed-chair speculation, Trump weak-dollar comments). On a peace-dividend day with S&P record, gold should be flat — fact that gold is RISING means dollar-weakness regime DOMINANT, not crisis. Disambiguation ~85% dollar / ~15% war-risk residual. Silver +2.9%, copper +1.0%, platinum +1.6% confirm USD weakness mechanical. Decision-relevant binary: dollar-driven gold = bullish EM tailwind incl India once RBI eases; war-risk-driven gold = bearish for risk assets incl India.
